notes: run=manage sense → tmexxi (not tiġri). Pro-drop: no hi. Adjectival phrase ta' suċċess kbir follows noun.
run=movement → tiġri. Pro-drop. kull filgħodu = every morning.

source_en: The programme is running on my computer.
reference_mt: Il-programm qed jaħdem fuq il-kompjuter tiegħi.
notes: run=operate/function → jaħdem (work/function sense). Definite article il- on both programme and computer.
run out of → spiċċa + clitic -lhom (indirect object). Subject l-ilma follows verb. Distinctive Maltese experiencer construction.
run for election → ħiereġ (emerge/come forward sense). għal + president. Active participle used for progressive.
bank=financial. mort (went). il-bank without explicit preposition — location verbs in Maltese drop 'to' before definite noun.
bank=xatt (river bank/shore). tax-xmara = ta' + ix-xmara (genitive contraction). mimli tajn = full of mud (adjective phrase).

source_en: I need to book a flight.
notes: need → għandi bżonn (I have need). book=verb → nibbukkja (loan from English, non-semitic). titjira = flight (indefinite).
book (noun, plural) → kotba with definite article l-. taqra' = 3SF imperfect of qara. kull filgħaxija = every evening.

source_en: Can you turn on the light?
notes: can → tista'. turn on the light → tixgħel id-dawl (light up the light). Question formed by intonation only, no inversion.

source_en: The light bag is on the table.
notes: light=adjective (not heavy) → ħafif/ħafifa. bag → basket or borża. Adjective follows noun. qiegħed/qiegħda = placed/located (copula for location).
ma jistax = negated 3SM of seta'. jiġi = 3SM imperfect of ġie (come). llejla = tonight.

source_en: We are going to eat now.
notes: going to (near future) → sejrin (active participle of sar/mar, plural). nieklu = 1P imperfect of kiel. issa = now.

source_en: Do you know where she is?
notes: Embedded question: fejn qiegħda hi. No question inversion in Maltese embedded clause. taf = 2S imperfect of af (know). qiegħda = located (3SF).
Two negation strategies shown: mhux qed nifhem (negated progressive) vs ma nifhimx (direct negation suffix). il-mistoqsija = the question (definite, feminine).

source_en: They love swimming in the sea.
notes: love + gerund → iħobbu + imperfect complement jgħumu. fil-baħar = fi + il-baħar (fused preposition). No li connector needed with ħabb + verb.
miss (fail to catch) → tilfet (3SF perfect of tilef, lost/missed). ix-xarabank = the bus (assimilated article before x).
like → jogħġbu (impersonal: it pleases him) → negated ma jogħġbux. Subject il-ħaxix follows the verb. Experiencer construction — not he-likes-it but it-pleases-him.

source_en: We need to go to school.
notes: need → għandna bżonn. go to school → immorru l-iskola (no preposition before definite school). 1P imperfect of mar.
it-tfal = the children (assimilated article it- before t). qed jilagħbu = 3P progressive. barra = outside.
Existential weather: hawn (there is/it is) + noun phrase, NOT copular. kesħa = cold (noun/abstract quality, feminine). ħafna = much/very. No impersonal 'it' in Maltese.
ommi = my mother (possessive suffix -i). hija = she is (copula 3SF). tabiba = female doctor. No indefinite article in Maltese predicate nominals.
Past question: no did-auxiliary in Maltese. Verb-first question by intonation. kiltu = 2P perfect of kiel (also acceptable 2S: kilt). l-kolazzjon = the breakfast (definite).

source_en: I have two brothers and a sister.
reference_mt: Għandi żewġ aħwa subien u waħda tifla.
alternative_references_mt: Għandi żewġt aħwa u oħt waħda.
notes: żewġ = two (attributive, before plural noun). aħwa subien = brothers (siblings + masculine specifier). u waħda tifla = and one daughter/girl. Counting construction differs from simple listing.
Qalli = qal + lili (told + to-me), clitic fusion. storja = story (indefinite). Ditransitive collapsed onto verb via clitic.
